calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Set For Trial On Sep-03-09 At 10:35 A.M. In Dept 210

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Continued To Sep-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: 9:05am
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Appellant, Stephen Leach, appears in Pro Per; Respondent Mr. Imperial appears in Pro Per for NEXTARTS. This matter was continued to allow Mr. Imperial to arrange for a reporter. Mr. Imperial appears without a reporter and states he will not let a Pro Tem Judge hear the case. Mr. Leach objects to Mr. Imperial not stipulating to a Judge Pro Tem. The clerk informs Mr. Leach that if one party objects the case cannot be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. The case is then transferred to Dept. 206 for assignment to a Court judge.

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Continued To Sep-03-09 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Small Claims Appeal by Plaintiff Nextarts (Anthony Imperial)on Cross-complaint.

Appearances: Nextarts (Anthony Imperial)- Plaintiff (Petitioner)
Stephen Leach-Defendant (Respondent)

Background facts: The judgment was entered in Defendant's favor (Mr. Leach) in the amount of $501.53 principal, $59.00 costs and 0% interest on defendant's claim on July 7, 2009.

Ruling; Argued and the court took the matter under submission. Parties, submitted in open court respective documents.( Respondent defendant Mr. Leach, submitted Initial Invoice in the amount of $500+ & Contract. Petitioner plaintiff, Nextarts likewise submitted copy of the contract) Judge R. Quidachay, Clerk R. Pascual. Dept. 501.

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Sep-30-2009 Text Ruling

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Continued To Sep-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES: 9:20am
Judge Pro Tem Deborah Daniloff, clerk-Beth Wilber, nothing on record, Dept. 525:
Cause called for Small Claims Appeal. Appellant, Stephen Leach, appears in Pro Per; Respondent Nextarts, appears in Pro Per through Anthony Imperial. Parties and witnesses are sworn. Mr. Imperial requested that the Court provide a reporter. He was informed by the Court that he must arrange for his own reporter in a small claims hearing or appeal. Mr. Imperial also suggested he would withdraw his stip to allow Judge Daniloff to hear the case, stating he was not sure she understood the law re small claims appeals. The matter, however, was continued to Sep-03-09, 9:00am, to allow Mr. Imperial to seek legal advice as how to arrange for a court reporter.
9:30am
Matter is submitted, Appellant's exhibits are retained. Plaintiff's exhibits were not in the file, and no new evidence was introduced be either party.
